content: SOURCE: Generated Data based on JNTUH affiliated college admission procedures and standard Telangana engineering college admission practices. ================================================================ GNITS ADMISSIONS INFORMATION G. Narayanamma Institute of Technology and Science, Hyderabad ================================================================ ABOUT GNITS ------------ G. Narayanamma Institute of Technology and Science (GNITS) is a premier women's engineering college located in Shaikpet, Hyderabad, Telangana. Established in 1997, it is affiliated to Jawaharlal Nehru Technological University Hyderabad (JNTUH) and approved by AICTE. The college is NAAC accredited and offers undergraduate and postgraduate programs. Address: Pjts Nagar, Shaikpet, Hyderabad, Telangana - 500008 Phone: 040-23004000 Email: admissions@gnits.ac.in Website: gnits.ac.in PROGRAMS OFFERED ----------------- Undergraduate (B.Tech) - 4 Years: 1. EAMCET (Engineering Agriculture and Medical Common Entrance Test) - Telangana State - Conducted by TSCHE (Telangana State Council of Higher Education) - Based on rank in TS EAMCET exam - 70% seats (Category A) filled through EAMCET counseling 2. Management Quota (Category

State - Conducted by TSCHE (Telangana State Council of Higher Education) - Based on rank in TS EAMCET exam - 70% seats (Category A) filled through EAMCET counseling 2. Management Quota (Category B) - 30% seats filled by college management - Based on merit in qualifying examination and interview 3. NRI Quota - For Non-Resident Indian students - Apply directly to college with required documents CATEGORY A SEATS (EAMCET): Step 1: Appear for TS EAMCET (usually in May) Step 2: Download hall ticket and rank card from TS EAMCET website Step 3: Wait for counseling schedule published by TSCHE Step 4: Register for web counseling on tsche.ac.in Step 5: Pay counseling fee (Rs. 1200 for OC, Rs. 600 for BC/SC/ST) Step 6: Exercise web options (preferred colleges and branches) Step 7: Seat allotment announced based on rank, category, and preferences Step 8: Report to college with documents for admission CATEGORY B SEATS (MANAGEMENT): Step 1: Contact GNITS admissions office directly Step 2: Fill

announced based on rank, category, and preferences Step 8: Report to college with documents for admission CATEGORY B SEATS (MANAGEMENT): Step 1: Contact GNITS admissions office directly Step 2: Fill application form (available at college or online) Step 3: Submit required documents Step 4: Appear for merit/interview if required Step 5: Fee payment and confirmation ELIGIBILITY CRITERIA --------------------- B.Tech: - Must have passed 10+2 (Intermediate) with Mathematics, Physics, and Chemistry - Minimum 45% aggregate in PCM (40% for reserved categories) - Must have valid TS EAMCET rank (for Category A seats) - Age: 17 years as on December 31 of admission year M.Tech: - B.Tech/B.E. in relevant branch from recognized university - Minimum 50% aggregate (45% for reserved categories) - Valid GATE score preferred (mandatory for JNTUH stipend) - TS PGECET rank for state quota seats MBA: - Any bachelor's degree with minimum 50% aggregate - Valid ICET (Integrated Common Entrance Test) score for

score preferred (mandatory for JNTUH stipend) - TS PGECET rank for state quota seats MBA: - Any bachelor's degree with minimum 50% aggregate - Valid ICET (Integrated Common Entrance Test) score for Telangana state seats - Must have completed 10+2+3 years of education MCA: - Bachelor's degree in relevant field with Mathematics as one subject - Minimum 50% aggregate - Valid ICET score REQUIRED DOCUMENTS FOR ADMISSION ---------------------------------- Original Documents: 1. Students must maintain a minimum of 75% attendance in each subject to be eligible for semester end examinations. academics What happens if my attendance falls below 75%? Students with less than 75% attendance will not be permitted to appear in semester end examinations for that subject. academics Can attendance shortage be condoned at GNITS? Yes. Students with attendance between 65% and 75% can apply for condonation with valid medical or other genuine reasons. Maximum 10% condonation is granted. academics How is attendance calculated at GNITS? Attendance is calculated separately for theory and laboratory subjects from the first day of class to the last working day of the semester. academics Can parents view my attendance online? Yes. 1.3 GRADING SYSTEM (CBCS - Choice Based Credit System) -------------------------------------------------------- GNITS follows JNTUH CBCS grading system: Grade Points: O (Outstanding): 91-100 marks = 10 grade points A+ (Excellent): 81-90 marks = 9 grade points A (Very Good): 71-80 marks = 8 grade points B+ (Good): 61-70 marks = 7 grade points B (Above Average): 51-60 marks = 6 grade points C (Average): 41-50 marks = 5 grade points P (Pass): 35-40 marks = 4 grade points F (Fail): Below 35 external = 0 grade points (Fail) Ab (Absent): Absent = 0 grade points SGPA (Semester Grade Point Average): SGPA = Sum of (Credit × Grade Point) / Sum of Credits in that semester CGPA (Cumulative Grade Point Average): CGPA = Sum of (Credit × Grade Point for all semesters) / Total Credits Credit Structure (B.Tech): - Theory subjects: 3 or 4 credits each - Laboratory subjects: 1.5 or 2 credits each - Project: 6 credits (final year) - Total credits for B.Tech: 160 credits approximately 1.4 ACADEMIC
